\newcommand{\QFortyOneAuditBlock}{%
The numerical orbit data in Lemma~\ref{lem:q41} and in the table below are reproducibly checked by the no-op command
\begin{center}
\QFortyOneCheckCommand
\end{center}
with parameters \(q=41\), \(p=41\), \(v=1723\), and \(\tau=1\).
This command rebuilds the payload in memory, compares it with \texttt{generated/q41\_frontier.tex}, and exits nonzero on mismatch.
To regenerate the checked-in TeX block, run \QFortyOneWriteCommand.
The generated checks give \(\#\Omega=574\), \(|C_A|=|C_B|=44\), residual count \(530\), and digest \QFortyOneGeneratedDigest.
The survivor scan rejects exactly those \(\tau\) for which some forced point \(\tau+s\) is \(0\),
or for which, after repeated forced point-orbits are identified, an internal or cross forced
difference-orbit has smaller than the expected size \(2\) or \(6\), or two forced
difference-orbits coincide.
}
\newcommand{\QFortyOneForcedCores}{%
It remains only to display the script-generated local forced cores for \(\tau=1\).  For
\(A\), the forced point-orbits are \([1],[2],[4],[12]\), and their
internal and cross difference-orbits are
\[
\begin{aligned}
{[1]}_{\rm int}&:\ [40],[83]\\
{[2]}_{\rm int}&:\ [80],[166]\\
{[4]}_{\rm int}&:\ [160],[332]\\
{[12]}_{\rm int}&:\ [480],[996]\\
[2]-[1]&:\ [1],[39],[42],[44],[124],[125]\\
[4]-[1]&:\ [3],[37],[46],[126],[206],[209]\\
[4]-[2]&:\ [2],[78],[84],[88],[248],[250]\\
[12]-[1]&:\ [11],[29],[54],[462],[534],[545]\\
[12]-[2]&:\ [10],[70],[96],[420],[576],[586]\\
[12]-[4]&:\ [8],[152],[180],[336],[660],[668]
\end{aligned}
\]
These are \(4\cdot2+6\cdot6=44\) distinct difference-orbits,
leaving \(574-44=530\) residual difference-orbits.

For \(B\), the forced point-orbits are \([1],[2],[5],[12]\), and the
corresponding list is
\[
\begin{aligned}
{[1]}_{\rm int}&:\ [40],[83]\\
{[2]}_{\rm int}&:\ [80],[166]\\
{[5]}_{\rm int}&:\ [200],[415]\\
{[12]}_{\rm int}&:\ [480],[996]\\
[2]-[1]&:\ [1],[39],[42],[44],[124],[125]\\
[5]-[1]&:\ [4],[36],[47],[168],[247],[251]\\
[5]-[2]&:\ [3],[77],[89],[126],[289],[292]\\
[12]-[1]&:\ [11],[29],[54],[462],[534],[545]\\
[12]-[2]&:\ [10],[70],[96],[420],[576],[586]\\
[12]-[5]&:\ [7],[193],[222],[294],[702],[709]
\end{aligned}
\]
Again all \(44\) listed orbits are distinct.  Thus the first forced
cores at \(q=41\) do not by themselves contradict the exact-cover
conditions; the remaining problem is a \(10\)-orbit completion problem.
}
